user_prompt: Morphix横竖转换(横→竖):把竞品BP二选一购买界面(高级/豪华通行证并列)翻成竖屏,保两档并列可比+各自奖励预览+价格购买;先只翻布局不换风格(X3风格留下一段)

params.prompt:
GAME UI LAYOUT REORIENTATION — redesign a game UI for a new aspect ratio, FOLLOWING the conventions of professional game UI for the TARGET orientation.

Reference image #1 = a game UI screen currently in LANDSCAPE (horizontal, wide): a Battle-Pass 'TWO-CHOICE' PURCHASE popup showing TWO passes side by side (a cheaper standard pass on the left and a premium 'deluxe' pass on the right), each with a reward-preview grid and its own price + purchase button — the player picks ONE of the two.

Your job: redesign the SAME UI for a PORTRAIT (vertical, tall) canvas. This is NOT a stretch, NOT a rotation, NOT a crop. It is a full layout REDESIGN, as a senior game UI designer would do.

PORTRAIT GAME UI LAYOUT CONVENTIONS (non-negotiable):
- TOP-LEFT: back/close button (X).
- TOP-RIGHT: player currencies in a row.
- TOP CENTER: title + subtitle below.
- MAIN CENTER: the primary content (the TWO comparable pass options) takes the largest area.
- BOTTOM CENTER: primary purchase/confirm button(s), centered.
- Decorative banner strips -> full-width header bar; MUST NOT float mid-screen.
- 4-6 column landscape grids -> 2-3 columns in portrait.
- Character/key art -> top or background, NOT forced center over content.

KEEP THE TWO-CHOICE COMPARISON STRUCTURE: the two passes stay visually PARALLEL and easy to compare — two side-by-side columns in portrait (or clearly separated stacked panels) — each keeping its reward preview + its own price + purchase button, so the player still picks ONE of TWO.

SCROLLABLE LISTS: show only what comfortably fits; the rest implicit/scrollable; keep the SAME per-item visual density, do not compress to fit all.

AXIAL / LINEAR COMPONENTS: flip their axis to match portrait's long side (vertical). A horizontal reward rail becomes vertical. Re-lay-out semantically, do NOT rotate pixels 90 degrees.

INTERNAL STEPS: inventory every element -> reposition each by portrait convention -> reflow grids to 2-3 columns -> keep the two-pass comparison -> move close button top-left -> center the purchase buttons -> rebalance whitespace.

STRICT: preserve every structural element; preserve text verbatim; preserve semantic roles.
FORBIDDEN: stretch/squeeze/distort; 90-degree rotation shortcut; crop/drop elements; float banners mid-screen; change any wording.

Match image #1's ORIGINAL visual style — only LAYOUT and POSITIONS change (the X3 restyle will happen in a later step).

Output: a polished, professionally laid-out game UI mockup in PORTRAIT (vertical, 9:16).
